attacking_style: Limited evidence of personal attacking; primarily operates as a high-altitude pacer and positioning specialist rather than a primary aggressor in decisive moments.
team_tactics: Deploys early on climbs as a high-tempo setter to control pace and position protected riders, then steps aside rather than defending or counter-attacking; calculated confidence in team leaders.
terrain_preference: Climbs where sustained pacing is required rather than explosive terrain; Amstel-style punchy finales expose tactical hesitancy and decision-making weaknesses.
vulnerability: Late-race tactical decision-making in small groups where proactive attacking required; durability concerns following crashes; fails to capitalize when team numerically superior.
key_patterns: Amstel Gold appearances show passivity in decisive moments, failing to attack from advantageous positions (2018, 2017).
Functions as expendable climb pacer for team leaders rather than personal GC/result focus (Giro 2018).
Crash history and limited racing raise durability questions in stage race contexts (2017).
form_trajectory: neutral: Transitioned to clear domestique role by 2018 Giro after tactical failures and crash setbacks in 2017; stable support function rather than leadership ambitions.
